title: common: safer use of `filepath.EvalSymlinks()` in `findBindir()`
body:
This patch is directly ported from [[1]] to resolve the exact same issue mentioned in that pull-request.

Why is this patch needed? Because in [[2]], `FindExecutablePeer()` was replaced with `FindHelperBinary()`, making the fix introduced in [[1]] no longer effective, resulting in a regression. To resolve the regression[[3]], `EvalSymlinksOrClean()` is added to `findBindir()`. The function call stack is now:

```
FindHelperBinary() -> findBindir() -> EvalSymlinksOrClean()
```

This should fix https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27763

/cc @l0rd 

xref: https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Main/pull/6335#issuecomment-3769785863

[1]: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25151
[2]: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27612
[3]: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27763

<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

comments.body: @chawyehsu, thank you for fixing this. And for updating the PR after @Luap99 feedback. However, I am afraid you went a little bit too far: Duplicating `findBindir` per OS is overkill. You can keep one shared `findBindir` that calls `EvalSymlinksOrClean`. But the implementation of `EvalSymlinksOrClean`, for OSes other than Windows, should be a simple call to `filepath.EvalSymlinks`.

> Duplicating findBindir per OS is overkill.

It should be more concise now.

> > Duplicating findBindir per OS is overkill.
> 
> It should be more concise now.

That's great, thank you. Do you mind adding a Windows-specific unit test? You should be able to reuse the code that you removed from the [Podman PR](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27968).
@mheon @Luap99 do we still have time to add this to 5.8? Currently users who install Podman using Scoop [fail to start the Podman machine](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27763).

> @mheon @Luap99 do we still have time to add this to 5.8? Currently users who install Podman using Scoop https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27763.

yes we still have time, once this merges we can backport it to the podman-v5.8 branch here

btw as for windows unit tests we do not have windows CI here, and I rather avoid dragging in the full windows CI systems here. But sure having the tests so it can be run locally is still useful I think.

> btw as for windows unit tests we do not have windows CI here, and I rather avoid dragging in the full windows CI systems here. But sure having the tests so it can be run locally is still useful I think.

Yes, that helps me validating the PR locally (tests fail when using `filepath.EvalSymlinks` and pass with this patch on Windows).
